what_it_does: This file is a channel-memory attachment record that links an image asset to a design-source pitch via metadata.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Target is a custom URI image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-41
No descriptive caption or title is present
standouts: Uses a custom image:// URI scheme to point at the attached image target
Serves as a typed attachment record (images) for a design-source pitch
Contains only machine-readable metadata with no human-readable explanation
conventions: Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
Uses a custom URI scheme for asset targeting
concerns: No caption or description of the image content
Target URI cannot be verified from this file alone
No author or provenance metadata beyond the timestamp
Empty body means no context if metadata is orphaned
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that ties an image asset to design-source pitch p-41 with a timestamp.
questions: What image content does image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-41 represent?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is the target image currently accessible or versioned?
What is the relationship between this attachment and pitch p-41?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose as an attachment metadata record matches what the file does.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to record that a specific image is attached to a design-source pitch so the asset can be retrieved in context later.
novelty: The custom image:// URI target scheme is the most distinctive feature; otherwise it is a conventional metadata attachment record.
contribution: It contributes a precise, timestamped provenance record that links a channel, an image asset, and a target pitch, which a fused system could use to reconstruct attachment history.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file that records an image asset linked to design-source pitch p-41 via a custom image URI and a timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to register an image attachment for a design-source pitch so it can be referenced and retrieved within channel memory.
